title: osmunda regalis subsp. palustris
intro: Osmunda regalis subsp. palustris, also known as the royal fern or marsh fern, is a subspecies of fern that belongs to the genus Osmunda, characterized by its large, lacy fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds. The health benefits of Osmunda regalis subsp. palustris include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote tissue repair, and support the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin issues and infections. Herbal preparations of Osmunda regalis subsp. palustris can be made by infusing the dried fronds in hot water or by using them in topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
